name: Washington University Sentence Completion Test
source: Hy, L.X. & Loevinger, J. (1996). Measuring Ego Development, 2nd ed. Lawrence Erlbaum.
sentence_stems: The thing I like about myself is...
If I can't get what I want...
When they talked about sex, I...
What gets me into trouble is...
When a child will not join in group activities...
When I am with a man...
note_on_stems: 25 of 36 stems compiled from published sources. Complete Form 81 available in Hy & Loevinger (1996). Male/female forms differ by pronoun gender in some stems.

stages.name: Impulsive

dimensions.impulse_control: Focused on bodily feelings, basic impulses, and immediate needs; minimal control
dimensions.interpersonal_style: Dependent, demanding, egocentric; dichotomous (people are 'good' if they meet needs, 'bad' if not)
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Bodily impulses, particularly sexual and aggressive ones

stages.scoring_characteristics: Responses are short, concrete, impulsive. Language reflects immediate gratification needs. Dichotomous good/bad evaluations.

dimensions.impulse_control: More sophisticated use of awareness of rules and consequences; manipulative; externalizes blame
dimensions.cognitive_style: Cause-and-effect reasoning applied for personal gain

I don't care / I get even

dimensions.interpersonal_style: Very invested in belonging to and obtaining approval of important reference groups
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Material things, reputation, status, appearance, adjustment; group membership
dimensions.cognitive_style: Greatest cognitive simplicity; right way and wrong way; simple, conventional, rule-bound, moralistic; stereotypical thinking

is what a woman should do

stages.name: Self-Aware

dimensions.interpersonal_style: Recognizes uniqueness in self and others; tension between 'real me' and 'expected me'
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Personal beliefs on religion, morality, relationships; unique feelings versus group norms
dimensions.cognitive_style: Wondering, questioning; increased self-criticism; awareness of multiple possibilities

stages.population_note: Most common stage among US adults. Few progress to this before age 25.

it depends on whether it's fair
I worry too much about what others think

stages.name: Conscientious

dimensions.impulse_control: Guided by self-evaluated standards; guilt-based rather than shame-based
dimensions.interpersonal_style: Increasing concern with mutuality and empathy in relationships
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Responsibility, achievement, long-term goals, personal ideals; standards are self-chosen and distinguished from manners
dimensions.cognitive_style: Greater conceptual complexity; differentiated emotional expression; people seen in terms of motives, not just actions

stages.key_shift: Adolescent or adult abandons convention for internal standards. Guilt comes from hurting others rather than breaking rules.

necessary guidelines, but I evaluate them against my own principles
I consider whether it's valid and try to learn from it
is a profound responsibility that requires self-examination
understands his children's individual needs and supports their growth

dimensions.impulse_control: Conflict awareness without resolution; awareness of inner paradoxes
dimensions.interpersonal_style: Broad-minded tolerance and respect for autonomy of both self and others; relationships valued more than achievement
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Heightened sense of individuality; concern for emotional dependence; inner conflicts and paradoxes
dimensions.cognitive_style: Vivid, unique self-expression; moralism replaced by awareness of inner conflict; greater psychological sophistication

reflections of a society's values, but they don't always capture the complexity of individual situations
I notice I have conflicting reactions — part of me wants to defend myself and part wants to hear the truth
that I sometimes lose myself trying to be true to both my own needs and those of the people I love

stages.name: Autonomous

dimensions.impulse_control: Integrated tolerance of inner conflict; freed from oppressive demands of conscience
dimensions.interpersonal_style: Relationships appreciated as an interdependent system of mutual support; emotional interdependence recognized as inevitable
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Individuality, self-actualization, autonomy; coping with inner conflict as the characteristic moral issue
dimensions.cognitive_style: High toleration for ambiguity and conceptual complexity; capacity to embrace polarity, complexity, and multiple facets; synthesizers who can conceptually integrate ideas

stages.key_capacity: Conscious recognition of opposing demands in personal complexity rises to awareness. Conflicts accepted as inevitable expressions of the fluid and multifaceted nature of people.

one expression of the ongoing tension between individual freedom and collective responsibility — both are necessary and neither can fully prevail
I try to hold the discomfort while really listening, knowing that growth often comes from the hardest feedback

stages.name: Integrated

dimensions.impulse_control: Reconciliation of inner conflicts; peace with unsolvable issues
dimensions.interpersonal_style: Wisdom and broad empathy towards oneself and others
dimensions.conscious_preoccupation: Full sense of identity; seeks integration and self-actualization across multifaceted self-aspects; reconciliation to one's destiny
dimensions.cognitive_style: Holistic, integrative; strong moral principles; capacity to cherish individuality while reconciling inner conflicts

stages.population_note: Rarely attained — less than 1% of US adults

part of the dance between order and freedom that makes human community possible — I hold them lightly but respectfully

scoring_method.item_level: Each of the 36 stems has its own scoring manual. Each response is independently rated on the E2-E9 scale using an exemplar-based method: a completion is scored by matching it to real examples grouped into categories by thematic similarity.

total_protocol_rating.ogive_rules: Item scores are arranged in a cumulative frequency distribution, and threshold values (Tk) are applied to classify the respondent. The ogive reflects that people maintain the capacity to respond at lower levels after leaving them behind.
total_protocol_rating.total_weighted_score: A mathematical sum giving higher weights to higher scores, yielding a continuous measure rather than discrete levels.
total_protocol_rating.mode_plus_impressionistic: Calculate the mode of item ratings, generate an impressionistic assessment, compare against frequency distributions. Tie-breaking rules apply when they diverge.

linguistic_correlates.self_centeredness: First-person singular pronouns decrease at higher development levels
linguistic_correlates.complexity: Conjunctions like 'but' and 'although' increase with maturation
linguistic_correlates.informality: Words reflecting leisure, anger, swearing, sexual content correlate with lower ego levels
linguistic_correlates.verbosity: Response length shows greatest gains at later developmental stages
